title: The Spirit Cabinet
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A cynical former stage magician investigates a fraudulent medium in post-WWI London and discovers that some spirits cannot be reasoned with or debunked.
long_description: London, 1921. Thomas Gale, a cynical former stage magician turned investigator for the London Rationalist Society, is dispatched to expose Madame Seraphina, a rising medium whose séances have captivated wealthy grieving families still reeling from the Great War. Posing as a bereaved uncle, Thomas documents every trick: hidden wires, luminous gauze, ventriloquist's palming. But when a séance attendee dies in a locked room during a sitting, Thomas discovers that Seraphina herself is terrified — convinced that something genuine and predatory has latched onto her performances. As the body count rises and Scotland Yard circles closer, Thomas must confront the possibility that a fraud can summon a truth she never believed in, and that some spirits cannot be reasoned with or debunked.
